commands.review_summary:
The PR advertises FeatureRunSession for the Cloudflare provider, returns RunSessionHandle values from post-lease Run paths, and adds Cloudflare provider tests for spec advertisement, keep, and keep-on-failure session behavior.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction. Source inspection verifies current main rejects --lease-output for Cloudflare because it lacks FeatureRunSession, and the PR tests simulate the intended provider behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for live Cloudflare proof and the RunID convention; there is no narrow automated repair because the patch has no concrete code finding.

target.title: feat(cloudflare): advertise FeatureRunSession + return run-session handle
target.body:
Per the run-session adoption tracker #182 (and @steipete's suggested next step there: *"audit those remaining delegated providers in small PRs, adding `FeatureRunSession` only where `crabbox run --provider <provider> --keep --lease-output session.json -- true` produces a usable cleanup handle and has real behavior proof"*), this is the first such per-provider PR.

`cloudflare` was the cleanest candidate: the lease lifecycle already exists and is exercised end-to-end by the existing httptest harness ŌĆö this PR only adds the missing run-session plumbing.

## What was already there
- **acquire** ŌĆö `createSandbox` (`backend.go`)
- **reuse** ŌĆö `resolveSandboxID` (claim / id)
- **keep** ŌĆö `shouldStop := acquired && !req.Keep`
- **keep-on-failure** ŌĆö `handleDelegatedRunFailure` already wired on both failure branches
- **cleanup** ŌĆö `Stop()` ŌåÆ `destroySandbox` (`DELETE /v1/sandboxes/{id}`)

## What this PR adds
- Advertise `core.FeatureRunSession` in the provider spec.
- Build a `RunSessionHandle` (`provider`, `leaseId`, `slug`, `reused`, `kept`, `runId`, `cleanupCommand`) once after `shouldStop` is computed, carried on **every** post-identity return path, with `Kept` re-stamped after each `handleDelegatedRunFailure`.
- Cleanup command mirrors the sibling `cloudflaredynamicworkers` convention: `crabbox stop --provider cloudflare --id <leaseId>` (cloudflare's `Stop` resolves `--id`). `RunID == leaseID`, same as the sibling.

Flag + `Session`-on-all-paths + tests land in one commit on purpose ŌĆö advertising the feature without populating `RunResult.Session` would fail `--lease-output` at the `did not return a session handle` guard.

## Behavior proof (no live creds ŌĆö uses the existing httptest harness)
- `TestCloudflareProviderSpec` ŌĆö spec advertises `run-session`.
- `TestCloudflareRunKeepReturnsSessionHandle` ŌĆö `--keep` returns a handle with `reused=false`, `kept=true`, and a **byte-exact** cleanup command; no destroy call.
- `TestCloudflareRunKeepOnFailureRetainsSession` ŌĆö `--keep-on-failure` on a failing command retains the lease (`kept=true`, no destroy).

`go build ./...`, `go vet`, and `go test ./internal/providers/cloudflare/... ./internal/providers/all/... ./internal/cli/...` all pass.

## One question for @steipete
I set `RunID == leaseID` to match `cloudflaredynamicworkers`. Shout if you'd prefer it left empty for cloudflare.

Follow-ups from the same audit (separate small PRs): `e2b`, `modal`, `wandb`, `upstash-box` look READY by the same pattern; `azure-dynamic-sessions` and `tensorlake` need the full wiring; `railway` is **not** a fit (service-control, not provisioning).

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
